what_it_does: Declares a module-level AI_TIC dict for 'framework-imposition': imposing a standard framework/terminology/structure onto content that has its own native shape. Carries id, label, markers, and description.
observations: Docstring states Tim actively rejects standard frameworks because they constrain thinking and miss relational patterns.
AI_TIC keys: id ('framework_imposition'), label ('framework-imposition'), markers (5 phrases), desc.
markers: "best practice", "industry standard", "framework", "design pattern", "according to the standard".
Docstring contains an explicit invariant: 'id MUST equal the file stem.'
standouts: Explicit 'id MUST equal the file stem' rule stated inline in the docstring.
Markers target generic-authority language (best practice, industry standard).
conventions: Same seed-as-data dict shape as sibling ai_tics files.
notes: A seed file defining the 'framework-imposition' tic — flagging generic-framework language that overrides Tim's native meaning.
questions: Is the bare marker 'framework' prone to false positives against legitimate uses?
purpose_vs_actual: Match — declares the framework-imposition seed tic as the docstring describes.
apparent_themes: Tim's framework over generic AI conventions
intention_signals: Encodes Tim's rejection of imposed standard frameworks as detectable markers so corpus text echoing generic frameworks can be flagged.
novelty: Casting 'best practice'/'industry standard' vocabulary itself as an anti-pattern to detect.
contribution: Contributes the 'framework-imposition' tic definition and its generic-authority markers, plus the stated id-equals-stem invariant.
summary_for_embedding: A Python seed file defining the 'framework-imposition' AI-tic dict with markers for generic-framework/standard language.
intention_for_embedding: To detect and subtract generic-framework/standard-terminology imposition that overrides the content's native shape.
